title: ranunculus gelidus subsp. grayi
intro: Ranunculus gelidus subsp. grayi, also known as Gray's buttercup, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the Ranunculaceae family. The plant is native to the mountainous regions of North America and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The health benefits of Gray's buttercup include its ability to treat skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues due to its salicylic acid content and other bioactive compounds. Traditionally, the plant has been used to prepare herbal remedies, such as salves and infusions, to promote healing and alleviate symptoms.
